title: Heliothis: a Global Problem
abstract:
The geographical distribution of the major pests, Heliothis armigera, H. zea, and H. vlrescens,
and the crop losses caused by these are reviewed. Although i t is generally considered that
the destruction of natural enemies by pesticide use and changes In cropping patterns and
management have promoted these insects to malor pest status, there are areas where H. armlgera
Is a serious pest, although traditional agriculture is s t i l l practiced, and no pesticides
are used. The dangers of a further increase in losses to Heliothis spp by breeding more
susceptible crops are described. There is a need for a more imaginative and holistic approach
to research directed towards the management of these pests.
